name: tldr
desc: Simplified, community-driven man pages that provide practical, real-world examples for command-line tools.
use_case: Quickly looking up syntax for complex commands like tar or ffmpeg instead of reading long manual pages.

name: bat
desc: A cat clone with syntax highlighting, git integration, and automatic paging for cleaner file viewing in terminal.
use_case: Reading code files directly from the command line with clear color themes and git status markers.

name: htop
desc: An interactive, text-based process viewer and system monitor designed to replace the standard top command.
use_case: Monitoring CPU core loads, memory utilization, and identifying frozen or resource-heavy applications.
